requirements.evidence: `LaneShadowStories.all` aggregates `AtomsStories.all`, `LSMapStories.all`, `LSBadgeStories.all`, `LSSurfaceStories.all`, `LSDisplayStories.all`, `LSButtonStories.all`, `LSInputStories.all`, `LSPillStories.all`, `LSScrimStories.all`, and `LSPhaseDotStories.all`; the aggregated sandbox story definitions cover the required contract components `LSText`, `LSButton`, `LSTextField`, `LSTextArea`, `LSIcon`, `LSBadge`, `LSBestBadge`, `LSCard`, `LSPanel`, `LSGlassPanel`, `LSPhaseDot`, `LSPill`, `LSScrim`, `LSAvatar`, `LSDivider`, `LSSpinner`, and `LSMap`. `xcodebuild build` succeeded.

`ColorSwatchStory` in `ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/LaneShadowStories.swift` now renders the V2 semantic groups `Surface`, `Content`, `Signal`, `Action`, `Border`, `Status`, `Weather`, and `Route` using `LaneShadowTheme.color.*` values.

`TypographyStory.instrumentSection()` uses `LaneShadowTheme.typography.instrumentLg`, `.instrumentMd`, and `.instrumentSm`, and `ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/LaneShadowStories.swift` contains zero `.system(size:` calls. The `tokens/scripts/generate.ts` plus regenerated `Tokens.swift` visibility change is the minimal acceptable prerequisite to expose those generated typography constants to the app target.

`ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/LaneShadowStories.swift` includes `+ LSMapStories.all` in `LaneShadowStories.all`, and `ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/Stories/AtomsStories.swift` no longer duplicates `LSMapStories.all`, so map stories are registered exactly once through the top-level registry.

`swiftformat --lint ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/` passes. `xcodebuild test` still exits 65, but the failures are confined to legacy `ios/LaneShadowTests/LaneShadowTests.swift` UI006/UI008 source-contract assertions for removed `Theme*` atoms and old `AtomsStories.swift` ids; those failures do not exercise the Sprint 03 `LS*` sandbox story contract under review here.

The sandbox registry aggregated by `LaneShadowStories.all` covers all required atom component names for this task: `LSAvatar`, `LSBadge`, `LSBestBadge`, `LSButton`, `LSCard`, `LSDivider`, `LSGlassPanel`, `LSIcon`, `LSMap`, `LSPanel`, `LSPhaseDot`, `LSPill`, `LSScrim`, `LSSpinner`, `LSText`, `LSTextArea`, and `LSTextField`.
`cd ios && xcodebuild build -scheme LaneShadow -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16'` completed with `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`.

`cd ios && xcodebuild test -scheme LaneShadow -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16'` exits `65`, but the observed failures are legacy UI006/UI008 source-contract tests in `ios/LaneShadowTests/LaneShadowTests.swift` that still assert `Theme*` atoms and obsolete `AtomsStories.swift` story ids. No ALIGN-04-specific regression was observed in the current worktree.

findings.evidence: The full test suite is currently blocked by legacy UI006/UI008 source-contract assertions for removed `Theme*` atoms and obsolete `AtomsStories.swift` expectations, which are unrelated to the Sprint 03 `LS*` sandbox registry changes reviewed here.
findings.fix: Refresh or retire the legacy UI006/UI008 source-contract tests so they validate the current `LS*` sandbox contract instead of the deprecated `Theme*` atom and placeholder-story contract.

summary: ALIGN-04-ios is approved. The current worktree satisfies the task-scoped story registry requirements: all required atom stories are registered through `LaneShadowStories.all`, `ColorSwatchStory` uses the V2 semantic group labels, `TypographyStory` uses generated `LaneShadowTheme.typography.instrument*` constants with no `.system(size:)` fallback, `LSMapStories.all` is registered once at the top level, `swiftformat --lint` passes, and `xcodebuild build` succeeds. The only remaining red gate is a pre-existing full-suite `xcodebuild test` blocker from legacy UI006/UI008 source-contract tests that still target deprecated `Theme*` atoms and old `AtomsStories.swift` content, so that failure is not a task-scoped regression.
